HOW I FOUND AN 8(a) COMPANY TO CONSULT FOR (step-by-step prospecting guide for a GovCon consultant)

1. Find an active 8(a) firm in your state:
   a. Go to the SBA DSBS / PRO-Net search (web.sba.gov/pro-net/search/dsp_dsbs.cfm).
   b. Choose the state and make sure "Active 8(a)" is checked.
   c. To narrow by industry, type the NAICS code in the proper field.
   d. Under "Search Results Display Options" click Edit to open a new page.
   e. Click the headings you want shown in results, then scroll up and Save.
   f. Scroll down and click "Search Using These Criteria."
   This gives a LIST. Look for companies with INCOMPLETE profiles (a sign they may not be actively doing government contracts). Pay attention to the 8(a) exit date — some expire this year. Click into each to see the full profile, including contact info to call and email. Call first!

2. Confirm they're NOT currently doing contracts (so they're a good prospect):
   - Go to FPDS (fpds.gov).
   - Search by the company name as it appears on their profile, or better, by their DUNS/UEI number.
   - If you get "No results found" → BOOM, you have a prospect. Pick up the phone and call.

3. What to say when you call:
   - Ask for the owner; introduce yourself as a government-contract consultant; note you saw they're 8(a) certified and you'd like to help them find opportunities. Ask if they have a minute, or prefer another time, or suggest an in-person meeting.
   - If they accept, do homework first: look at how many contracting actions their NAICS code had in the past two years, review their SBA profile and highlight what they're missing, and even print a few real opportunities. Showing you did research about their company proves you know what you're talking about.
